author: Dr. Lizard
title: Why is distinguishing monoallelic from biallelic TP53 mutations clinically important in MDS?
answer: Only multi-hit or biallelic TP53 mutations predict poor outcomes; monoallelic mutations behave like wild-type.
detail: Monoallelic TP53 mutations have survival outcomes similar to TP53 wild-type MDS patients.
Biallelic or multi-hit TP53 mutations confer significantly worse prognosis and treatment resistance.
This distinction affects treatment decisions including transplant timing and clinical trial eligibility.
Approximately 66-78% of TP53-mutated MDS cases have biallelic inactivation requiring aggressive management.
releaseNote: Distinguishing between monoallelic and biallelic TP53 mutations is crucial in MDS as it impacts treatment decisions and prognosis.
